headline: The recommended educators converge on agent orchestration: agents that loop, coordinate, and reach beyond the model to do real work.
body: The dominant thread across the inner circle is moving agents from manual prompting to self-running orchestration. Cole Medin reports engineers behind Claude Code and OpenClaw now use loops where an orchestrator spawns parallel worker agents under dashboard observability and cost control, which compounds both capability and hallucination risk [3], while Cyril Imhof frames the same harness engineering and AI loops around Claude Fable 5 and regulated industries [6], and IBM Technology details agent orchestration, task coordination, and policy enforcement for production workflows [2]. Tooling fills the gaps around these agents: Agent-Reach gives agents live context from Western and Chinese platforms [1], Voicebox supplies local voice cloning and text-to-speech [4], and IBM's security podcast weighs letting agents detect and reset compromised passwords against Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday covering over 200 CVEs [5]. Outside the recommended set, Google Cloud Tech lays out event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation for long-running agents [11], and Caleb Writes Code grounds the stack with a bigram walkthrough of tokenization, embeddings, cross entropy, softmax, and AdamW [0].

cited_items.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
